# Plan: Single-File HTML To-Do App

## Overview

A self-contained, single-file HTML application for managing to-do tasks with localStorage persistence, responsive design, and smooth animations using gray tones.

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- Single HTML file containing embedded CSS and JavaScript
- Task creation with text input
- Task completion toggle (visual: gray out + strikethrough)
- Delete functionality for completed tasks (trash icon)
- Browser localStorage for data persistence
- Responsive layout (mobile and desktop)
- Smooth animations and transitions
- Gray-tone color palette

**Out of Scope:**
- Multi-user support or authentication
- Backend/server integration
- Task editing after creation
- Task categories or tags
- Due dates or reminders
- Export/import functionality

## Technical Decisions

### Architecture
- **Single-file HTML**: All HTML, CSS, and JavaScript in one `index.html` file
- **No build tools**: Pure vanilla HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks or bundlers)
- **Storage**: Browser localStorage API for persistence
- **Structure**: Module pattern for JS organization within script tags

### Technology Stack
- **HTML5**: Semantic markup
- **CSS3**: Flexbox/Grid for layout, CSS animations for transitions
- **Vanilla JavaScript (ES6+)**: Modern JS features (arrow functions, template literals, destructuring)
- **localStorage API**: Browser-native persistence

### Design Principles
- **Mobile-first responsive design**: Works on all screen sizes
- **Gray tones palette**: Professional, calming aesthetic
- **Smooth animations**: Fade-ins, slide effects, transitions on state changes
- **Accessibility**: Semantic HTML, keyboard navigation support
- **Progressive enhancement**: Works without JS (form still renders)

### Code Organization
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>To-Do App</title>
  <style>
    /* All CSS here */
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <!-- HTML structure -->
  
  <script>
    /* All JavaScript here */
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

## High-Level Features

| FEAT-ID | Feature Name | Description | Status |
|---------|--------------|-------------|--------|
| FEAT-001 | HTML Structure & Base Styles | Create semantic HTML structure with gray-tone CSS styling and responsive layout | [ ] |
| FEAT-002 | Task Creation & Rendering | Implement task input form and dynamic task list rendering with data binding | [ ] |
| FEAT-003 | Task State Management | Add toggle completion (gray out + strikethrough) and delete functionality for completed tasks | [ ] |
| FEAT-004 | Data Persistence & Animations | Implement localStorage sync and smooth CSS animations for all interactions | [ ] |

## Success Criteria

1. **Functional**: Users can create, complete, and delete tasks; data persists across browser sessions
2. **Single-file**: Entire app contained in one HTML file (no external dependencies)
3. **Responsive**: Works seamlessly on mobile (320px+) through desktop (1920px+)
4. **Animated**: All state changes include smooth transitions (0.2-0.4s duration)
5. **Aesthetic**: Professional gray-tone palette with intuitive UI

## Dependencies

**None** — This is a standalone HTML file with no external libraries or frameworks.

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| localStorage size limits (5-10MB) | High | Display warning if storage quota exceeded; current design unlikely to hit limits |
| Browser compatibility (older browsers) | Low | Use ES6+ which works in all modern browsers (Chrome 51+, Firefox 54+, Safari 10+) |
| Data loss if localStorage cleared | Medium | Document this limitation; consider future enhancement for export/import |

## Testing Strategy

**Manual Testing** (no automated tests for this single-file app):
- Create tasks and verify they appear
- Complete tasks and verify visual changes
- Delete completed tasks
- Refresh browser and verify persistence
- Test on multiple screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Test keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Space)

## Documentation

- Inline code comments for key functions
- README with usage instructions and feature overview
- Technical documentation describing localStorage schema and state management approach

## Notes

- Since no `.spec-lite/memory.md` exists, this plan establishes baseline conventions
- Gray tones: Use palette like #f5f5f5, #e0e0e0, #9e9e9e, #616161, #212121
- Animations: Use CSS transitions for opacity, transform, background-color
- Icons: Use Unicode symbols (✓ for done, 🗑️ for trash) or CSS-generated icons to avoid external icon libraries
